root_motivation: Regressions in the MQO arm's weak failure modes (path_incompatible, wrong_date_role) are invisible — there is no stored baseline and no CI gate. Any change that quietly drops a mode's pass rate goes undetected until a human re-runs the scorer. This harness pins those numbers and makes regressions a build failure.
user_persona: Joe Yen (benchmark owner) and engineers shipping Components 1–4 of the MQO guardrail work, who run the harness locally and in CI to verify their changes improve — or at least don't worsen — the weak modes.

acceptance_criteria.description: All modes meet floor → exit 0, 'OK:' line in human output.
path_incompatible below floor → exit 1, report names failing mode.
Scoring semantics match score_path_correctness.py — PI keyword/fabrication/error+no-rows rules, dim check, calc check all match Python.
Mode absent from baseline is reported but not gated; present modes still gated.
--format json emits modes[], overall{any_below_floor, failing_modes}, tolerance.
Malformed input (bad JSON, nonexistent file) → exit 2 with stderr diagnostic.
cargo test passes; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; zero unsafe.
acceptance_criteria.test: CI: cargo test + cargo clippy -D warnings

scope: Score trajectories.jsonl against corpus, joining on task_id to get failure_mode
Baseline comparison with configurable tolerance; exit 0/1/2 contract
Human-readable table output and --format json machine-readable report
Failure-reason histogram per failing mode in human output
Seeded baseline.json file for 2026-06-09 MQO numbers
non_goals: Dispatching benchmark agents (orchestration stays in mcp-tuner)
Replacing score_path_correctness.py — this gates around it, not replaces it
Consuming path_verdict / filter_bind_report fields from Components 3–4 (v1 trajectory-only)
Storing historical run results or time-series tracking

five_whys_trace.q: Why do we need this CLI?
five_whys_trace.a: The mcp-tuner scorer produces per-mode numbers but there is no stored baseline and no exit-code contract CI can consume.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the absence of a stored baseline matter?
five_whys_trace.a: A change that drops path_incompatible from 22.5% to 10% would pass unnoticed — the only signal is a human re-running the scorer and remembering the old number.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is path_incompatible the critical mode?
five_whys_trace.a: At 22.5% it is the weakest mode and the direct target of Components 1–2 of the guardrail work; if those components regress it, the POC-readiness claim is invalidated.

ambiguities_resolved.question: OQ1: Tolerance band — exact floor or floor-minus-epsilon?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: --tolerance flag defaults to 0.0 (exact floor) but caller can set e.g. 0.05 to absorb k=4 variance. No default epsilon baked in.

ambiguities_resolved.question: OQ2: Consume path_verdict / filter_bind_report fields?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: v1 trajectory-only: only fields from the established schema. Richer attribution deferred to v2 once Components 3–4 output schema exists.
